rmu.settings.magicalLightDegrades.name: Magical Light Degrades
rmu.settings.magicalLightDegrades.hint: If disabled, magical light creates a sharp edge, providing its base illumination up to its radius and dropping instantly to pitch black beyond. If enabled, the light drops two tiers at the radius boundary and degrades normally thereafter, creating a softer fade.
rmu.settings.lightMapping.name: Light Mapping (Emitted Light)
rmu.settings.lightMapping.hint: Determines how RMU Light Tiers translate to the physical canvas radii.
rmu.settings.visionStrictness.name: Vision Strictness (Perceived Light)
rmu.settings.visionStrictness.hint: Determines how characters perceive dim environments (Shadowy/Dark tiers).
rmu.settings.visionStrictness.standard: Standard (Normal vision can see Dim areas)
rmu.settings.visionStrictness.gritty: Gritty (Normal vision treats Dim areas as Pitch Black; requires Nightvision)
rmu.settings.migrationMenu.name: Lighting & Vision Control Panel
rmu.settings.migrationMenu.label: Open Control Panel
rmu.settings.migrationMenu.hint: Apply RMU rules to the current scene, or disable the module and restore native Foundry settings.
rmu.migration.title: RMU Scene Migration
rmu.migration.description: Choose how you want to manage the tokens and lights on the current scene.
rmu.migration.appliedSuccess: RMU Lighting Engine successfully applied to current scene.
rmu.migration.restoredSuccess: Foundry VTT defaults successfully restored to current scene.
rmu.keybinds.checkLighting.name: Check Target Lighting
rmu.keybinds.checkLighting.hint: Outputs the exact RMU lighting degradation penalty between the selected token and their target.
rmu.warnings.selectOneTarget: You must control exactly one token and target exactly one token to calculate lighting penalties.
rmu.chat.noLineOfSight: No Line of Sight to Target
rmu.light.baseIlluminationHint: The starting light tier within the first 10 feet. The module will automatically degrade the light at 30, 100, and 300 feet.
rmu.light.isMagicalHint: Flags this light as magical, allowing it to overcome standard magical darkness.
rmu.light.isUtter.name: Is Utterlight/Utterdark?
rmu.light.isUtter.hint: Check this box to elevate the source to Utterlight (if positive luminosity) or Utterdark (if negative luminosity), making it absolute.
